subtitle: Kneecap pain, especially on downhills

mild.description: Dull ache around kneecap during or after runs
why: Kneecap tracking slightly off-centre due to quad imbalance
Weak VMO (inner quad) allowing lateral pull on patella
Foot pronation misaligning the entire kinetic chain
relief: Ice kneecap area for 15 min after runs
KT tape patella taping for run support
Straight leg raises to strengthen VMO without knee load
prevention: Step-ups and split squats to build VMO and glutes
Check for overpronation — stability shoes or orthotics
Increase cadence by 5–10% to reduce knee stress

moderate.description: Pain during runs that worsens going downhill or after sitting
moderate.can_run_note: Flat routes only, reduce pace, stop if pain worsens
why: Sustained cartilage irritation under the kneecap
Hip weakness causing inward knee collapse on landing
Training load exceeding tissue recovery capacity
relief: Focus on glute and hip strengthening daily
prevention: Single-leg squats — build to 3 × 15 each leg
Hip abductor strengthening: clamshells and lateral band walks
Gait analysis to identify lateral loading pattern
Build downhill volume gradually — max 10% grade initially

severe.description: Constant pain, swelling, pain climbing stairs
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — swimming or upper body only
why: Significant cartilage irritation or early chondromalacia
Possible synovial inflammation in the knee joint
relief: Complete running rest for 3–4 weeks minimum
Physiotherapy for manual therapy and tailored rehab
Consider imaging if no improvement after 4 weeks
prevention: Correct underlying hip and glute weakness before returning
Gait retraining if biomechanical fault identified

see_physio_if: Pain at rest or at night
No improvement after 3 weeks of self-management
